context: =  ADRENAL MEDULLARY PARAGANGLIOMA =  rare catecholamine-secreting tumor of chromaffin tissue; responsible for 0.1-0.9% of hypertensive individuals Prevalence:   0.13% in autopsy series; increasing occurrence of incidentalomas by cross- sectional imaging Incidence:   2-8÷1,000,000 annually Origin:   neuroectodermal tissue Path:   well-circumscribed 3-5 cm mass with central degenerative changes of fibrosis / cystic change; larger lesions are often hemorrhagic / cystic Histo:   chromaffin tumor cells contain chromogranin within secretory granule forming CHARACTERISTIC clusters of tumor cells = "
